# Venture Capital

<div class="blog-header">

## [How to Angel Invest, Part 2](https://nav.al/angel-2)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

A preview of our other podcast

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

We have another podcast called [Spearhead](http://spearhead.co), where
we discuss startups and angel investing. This is a compilation of recent
episodes. Also, see [Part 1](http://nav.al/angel-1). Everybody Thinks
They Already Have Good Judgment It takes years to know if you have good
judgment For the past few episodes, we talked about why you should angel
invest and how to get proprietary dealflow by building a
brand.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/angel-2" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [How to Angel Invest, Part 1](https://nav.al/angel-1)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

A preview of our other podcast

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

This is a preview of our [Spearhead](http://spearhead.co) podcast, where
we discuss startups and angel investing. Hey everybody, it’s
[Naval](http://twitter.com/naval) and [Nivi](http://twitter.com/nivi).
We’re going to talk about something very different than what we’ve
discussed in the past. Back in the day, we did [Venture
Hacks](https://venturehacks.com/), which was all about the game theory
of venture capital and helping entrepreneurs raise
money.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/angel-1" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

Jun 28 2013

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [A Venture SLA](https://nav.al/a-venture-sla)

</div>

<div class="content">

There is an opportunity for a new VC Firm to brand itself. Recent brands
in Venture Capital arose from transparency and founder-friendliness.
YCombinator gives new, young, technical talent an entry into Silicon
Valley. 500 Startups does it globally. Fred Wilson blogs the business.
Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz back Founders to be Public Company
CEOs.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/a-venture-sla" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Jun 28 2013

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Dec 1 2010

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [There is No Angel Bubble. There are Many Angel Bubbles.](https://nav.al/there-is-no-angel-bubble-there-are-many-angel-bubbles)

</div>

<div class="content">

A common meme floating around right now is that there is an Angel
investing bubble. In the sense that an enormous amount of capital is
being placed at risk, and its popping will have grave macro-economic
consequences, No. The total amount of additional capital flowing through
the Silicon Valley early-stage ecosystem, thanks to Super-Angels and
newly minted millionaires, is on the order of half-a-billion dollars or
so.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span> <a
href="https://nav.al/there-is-no-angel-bubble-there-are-many-angel-bubbles"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Dec 1 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [The Unbundling of the Venture Capital Industry](https://nav.al/the-unbundling-of-the-venture-capital-industry)

</div>

<div class="content">

People often mock Super-Angels as being impure because they invest other
people’s money. We also often get asked “What are VCs and Seed Funds
doing on AngelList?” (They’re clearly marked, by the way, and you can
choose who your pitch is visible to.) I will propose, however, that
whose money you’re investing is less relevant than on what terms it
comes on.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/the-unbundling-of-the-venture-capital-industry"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Dec 1 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Funding Markets Develop in Reverse](https://nav.al/funding-markets-develop-in-reverse)

</div>

<div class="content">

I co-founded AngelList because I was tired of saying no to
entrepreneurs. I wanted to say instead, “yes, we can help you get
funded.” So, it’s especially disappointing when we get promising
startups pitching on AngelList from remote locations. Some we’ve managed
to get funded – including ones in Canada and
Europe.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/funding-markets-develop-in-reverse"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Dec 1 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Apr 22 2010

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Why (Private) Investors are Herd Animals](https://nav.al/why-private-investors-are-herd-animals)

</div>

<div class="content">

It’s a common complaint – venture investors are driven by what other
investors think, and therefore lack imagination and spine. There’s some
truth to it – it is human nature, after all, to look for social proof
and authority when making decisions. However, that’s not the whole
story. In public markets, Investors make their decisions to invest in
parallel, and in theory, most of the relevant information about a
company is publicly disclosed, by
law.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/why-private-investors-are-herd-animals"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Apr 22 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Apr 10 2010

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Who has time for meetings?](https://nav.al/who-has-time-for-meetings)

</div>

<div class="content">

A lot of entrepreneurs assume that the initial way to engage with an
investor is to \*insist\* on a meeting. It’s a relatively safe
assumption that anyone on the buy side (an investor, an advertiser, an
executive at a large company) receives far more requests for meetings
than they can follow up on, and are constantly looking for excuses to
say “no.” Synchronous activities, such as phone calls, screencasts,
videos, and webex conferences are almost as
bad.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/who-has-time-for-meetings"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Apr 10 2010

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Jul 15 2007

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [STIRR discovers Price Discrimination](https://nav.al/at-stirr-events-vcs-fly-business-class-but-entrepreneurs-fly-coach-i-have-a-similar-rule-whenever-i-go-to-coffee-lunch-dinner-for-business-the-person-who-has-raised-more-money-pays8230)

</div>

<div class="content">

At STIRR events, VCs fly business class, but entrepreneurs fly coach 🙂
I have a similar rule – whenever I go to coffee / lunch / dinner for
business, the person who has raised more money
pays…<span class="post-more-link-before"></span> <a
href="https://nav.al/at-stirr-events-vcs-fly-business-class-but-entrepreneurs-fly-coach-i-have-a-similar-rule-whenever-i-go-to-coffee-lunch-dinner-for-business-the-person-who-has-raised-more-money-pays8230"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Jul 15 2007

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Apr 2 2007

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Introducing: Venture Hacks](https://nav.al/introducing-venture-hacks)

</div>

<div class="content">

I know I haven’t posted recently, but I have been busy!
[Nivi](http://www.nivi.com/blog) and I are launching a new site called
[Venture Hacks](http://www.venturehacks.com), an entrepreneur’s guide to
hacking Venture Capital. It’s a “tell all” site that helps entrepreneurs
get on an even footing with their better-informed counterparts when
negotiating an investment.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/introducing-venture-hacks"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Apr 2 2007

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>
